hawkish_phrases: two members preferred to maintain the current target range for the federal funds rate
President Rosengren dissented because he did not see a clear and compelling case for additional accommodation at this time given that the unemployment rate stood near 50-year lows
One member also noted that a further easing in policy at a time when the economy is very strong and asset prices are elevated could have adverse implications for financial stability
Several participants expressed the concern that capital ratios at the largest banks had continued to fall
A couple of participants expressed the concern that further monetary accommodation presented a risk to financial stability in certain sectors of the economy
dovish_phrases: In light of the implications of global developments for the economic outlook as well as muted inflation pressures, the Committee decided to lower the target range for the federal funds rate to 2 to 2-1/4 percent
inflation had continued to run below the Committee's 2 percent symmetric objective
A couple of participants indicated that they would have preferred a 50 basis point cut in the federal funds rate at this meeting rather than a 25 basis point reduction
promote a faster return of inflation to the Committee's symmetric 2 percent objective than would otherwise be the case
the prolonged shortfall in inflation from the long-run goal could cause inflation expectations to drift down
summary: The Committee voted 8-2 to cut the federal funds rate by 25 basis points to 2.00-2.25 percent, with two hawkish dissents from George and Rosengren, characterizing the action as a mid-cycle recalibration in response to muted inflation pressures and global uncertainties while maintaining flexibility for future adjustments.
